case.focal_claim: The study finds substantially higher income for low-caste households residing in villages dominated by BACs compared to villages dominated by upper caste.

execution_overview.overall_summary: Built a Python-based Docker image and executed both planned tasks. Addressed missing statsmodels by adding runtime pip installations and fixed a temporary syntax error. Results: domlow is positively associated with log income. One-sided t-test shows a strong positive difference. OLS coefficients are positive with marginal p-values near 0.05. Oaxaca decomposition indicates most of the mean difference is explained by covariates.

deviations.description: Added runtime pip installation for missing Python packages (statsmodels, patsy, etc.) within the analysis scripts.
Appended user site-packages to sys.path and invalidated import caches to ensure runtime-installed packages are importable.
deviations.reason: After installation, imports still failed within the container user environment.

deviations.description: Fixed a minor syntax error introduced during edits (duplicate import line).
deviations.reason: Script crashed with SyntaxError after previous edits.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: The planned analysis path (t-test, OLS with robust SEs across specifications, and Oaxaca decomposition) was preserved; only dependency and minor syntax fixes were applied.

test_statistics.other: T-test (one-sided) on log_totinc by domlow: t=5.1931, p=1.20e-07; Oaxaca twofold (Reimers 0.5): D=0.22484, Explained=0.20893, Unexplained=0.01591. Model2 domlow: coef=0.22636, p=0.05348; Model3 domlow: coef=0.16938, p=0.07505.

result_raw.result_text: Households in low-caste-dominated villages have higher log income. The one-sided mean difference test is strongly significant; OLS domlow coefficients are positive with marginal p-values near 0.05. Oaxaca suggests most of the mean difference is explained by covariates.

conclusion.conclusion_text: Evidence supports the claim: income is higher in low-caste-dominated villages. The mean difference is strongly significant, and regression estimates are positive and marginally significant.

deviations.description: Added runtime pip installation for Python packages inside the script.
deviations.reason: Missing statsmodels in the container environment.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: The restricted OLS with allowed controls was executed as planned; only dependency handling was added.

result_raw.result_text: In the restricted model, domlow is positively associated with log income with a marginal p-value (p=0.056).

conclusion.conclusion_text: The effect is in the supportive direction but does not reach the 0.05 significance threshold under the restricted controls.
